conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Sorry to hear that. So, what have you been up to lately in Atlanta?
Honestly, not much. Just trying to get through my daily routine as a physical therapist. You know, patient care, paperwork... (sigh)
I'm sure it's not always easy. How's your work situation right now?
It's tough. I love working with patients, but some days it feels like I'm just going through the motions.
That sounds frustrating. Do you find yourself losing interest in activities outside of work too?
Yeah, a bit. I used to love playing pickup basketball on weekends, but lately, even that seems like too much effort.
Sorry to hear that. How's your social life been? Any changes there?
Nah, not really. Still hanging out with the same crew from work and the gym.
That sounds nice. Are you finding yourself having trouble concentrating on tasks or making decisions lately?
All the time! I mean, simple things like planning a meal take me way longer than they used to.
I'm sure that's tough. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or eating habits?
Honestly? Yeah, I've been having trouble mustering up the energy to cook dinner sometimes. Takeout has become my go-to.
That can be convenient, but also maybe not as healthy. How about hobbies or things that bring you enjoyment?
To be honest? Not much these days. It's like everything feels like a chore.
I understand. Are there any recent stressful life events that might have triggered some of this for you?
Well, my best friend moved away a few months ago, and it really threw me off balance.
Sorry to hear that. Who do you go to for support when things get tough?
Mostly just my family and close friends, but sometimes I feel like they don't fully get what I'm going through.
It can be helpful to have a therapist who's not directly involved in your daily life. What are some goals you've set for yourself recently?
Honestly? Just trying to make it through each day without feeling too drained. Maybe getting back into exercising or something, but that feels really far away right now.
It sounds like you're just taking things one step at a time. That's totally understandable. What do you think is the biggest challenge for you in making progress?
Just being consistent, I guess. Like, some days it feels like I'm moving forward, and others... not so much.
That makes sense. It can be tough to sustain motivation. Let's take a look at what's been working well for you and build on that.
Yeah, okay. Maybe we can start with small steps, like getting back into my favorite TV show or something.
Sounds like a plan! What do you think would be the first step in getting started with that?
Hmm... maybe just setting aside 30 minutes each day to relax and watch an episode? That doesn't feel too overwhelming.
That's a great place to start. Can you tell me what you're hoping to achieve from our sessions here?
Honestly? Just being able to enjoy life again without feeling like I'm just going through the motions. Maybe gaining some confidence back in my ability to tackle challenges.
That sounds like a wonderful goal. Let's work together to make that happen. What do you think we should focus on in our next session?
Maybe exploring ways to boost my energy levels? That seems like it would help with the whole motivation thing.
Absolutely! We can definitely explore some strategies for increasing your energy. Before we wrap up today, is there anything that's been on your mind lately that you haven't had a chance to share?
Actually, I have been feeling guilty about taking time off work to come here. Like, what if my patients suffer or something?
Ah, guilt can be a heavy burden. Can you tell me more about what's behind those feelings of guilt?
Yeah... maybe just the fear of not being able to keep up with patient care. Or that I'm taking time away from them.
That makes sense. It sounds like there might be some underlying worries about your professional identity as a physical therapist. Let's explore that further in our next session, okay?
Yeah, okay. Sounds good.
